$schema_note: Podcast Production Engine (Skill 58) SACRED deterministic QC prover config, seeded from the Skill 57 bands pattern (57-social-media-in-a-box/config/bands.json). SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH: qc-tier1-mechanical.py reads THIS file (never hardcodes the numbers) and measures deterministically at zero model cost. Two sections. 'bands' holds numeric SACRED bands measured from the episode artifact's declared numeric fields (a band is the DEFAULT floor; a logged client-exact override wins and is recorded on the certificate; a client-exact number is NEVER floored or capped). 'tier1_checks' holds the parameters for the deterministic boolean Tier 1 hard-fail checks (CHECKLIST Part B, checks 1 to 11, 15, 16); the three SEMANTIC checks (12 fabrication, 13 mode perspective, 14 pronoun correctness) are NOT deterministic and run on the cheap judge tier per models.json, never here. A violation is a named AF-POD-* autofail. max:null means an open upper bound (a MINIMUM floor). Absent artifact fields are skipped (the prover measures only what the artifact declares). Zero em dashes; no triple-backtick fences.

episode_spoken_words.note: 7 to 15 minutes at 140 words per minute; 10-minute sweet spot is about 1400. Measured with Fish tags stripped (CHECKLIST Part B checks 8 and 9). Thin material means a tight short episode, never padding.

episode_runtime_seconds.note: 7 to 15 minutes; ffprobe-measured true duration, honestly reported.

words_per_minute_target.note: Pacing target used to size the word budget.

tag_sentences_per_tag.note: One Fish Audio tag roughly every two to five sentences, concentrated at pivots.

tag_min_per_paragraph.note: At least one delivery tag per paragraph (density floor, mirrors Skill 57 podcast_tag_density).

cover_px.note: Square cover; within 1400 to 3000; NEVER below 1400 square.

audio_lufs_integrated.note: Skill 23 loudness doctrine: integrated loudness between minus 16 and minus 14 LUFS. Values are negative; the prover checks min less-than-or-equal measured less-than-or-equal max.

book_teaser_pages.note: Interview mode ONLY (Personal mode skips the teaser entirely). At most three pages.

book_teaser_font_pt.note: Book-typeset PDF, no font below 14 point.

episode_package_font_pt.note: Episode Package rich document, no font below 12 point.

rubric_dimension_floor.note: Each of the 10 quality-rubric dimensions must score 8 or higher; NO averaging. This is the numeric floor the episode gate enforces after all 16 Tier 1 checks pass.

research_case_studies.note: Up to three case studies, each real and verified, demographic-matched where applicable; fewer honest studies beat padded ones.

power_statements.note: Three power statements extracted in the respondent's voice.

key_takeaways.note: Three key takeaways generated where missing.

research_calls_cap.note: Mirrors furnace.json web_research_calls_per_episode; the research-tool wrapper enforces the cap, the prover confirms the frozen package respected it.

tier1_checks.$note: Parameters for the deterministic boolean Tier 1 hard-fail checks (CHECKLIST Part B). Each maps to its Part B check number. Any single failure means the episode is NOT deliverable. Style-specific and reference-specific lists that the STYLE-ENGINE modules own (their four reference speakers, books, and talks) are contributed by those modules at runtime and merged over the seeds here; this config-set seeds only the settled, universally known parameters.

em_dash.note: Zero em dash (U+2014) characters anywhere in the deliverable. En dash and horizontal-bar variants are flagged as advisory to catch look-alikes.

code_fences.note: No code-fence markers of any kind (three-backtick or three-tilde). The three-backtick marker is stored escaped so the produced JSON bytes never contain a literal fence; it decodes to the three-backtick string the prover matches.

no_markdown.note: No markdown in the script: no bold or italic asterisks or underscores, no headers, no bullets, no numbered lists, no blockquotes.

no_labels.note: No Title:, section labels, speaker prefixes, Music or SFX placeholders, or plain-text stage directions.

title_placement.note: The word Title never precedes the title; a spoken title is woven into natural speech.

speakable_characters.note: Numbers, symbols, abbreviations, and units must be written as spoken. Flag bare digits, currency symbols, percent signs, and common abbreviations left unspoken.

s2_1_pro.note: S2.1 Pro delivery tags are free-form square brackets.

s1_legacy.note: S1 legacy uses parentheses; only when the account is specified S1.

tag_syntax.rule: No orphaned or malformed brackets; every opening delimiter has a matching close for the target model.

tag_count_exclusion.note: Word and character counts are recomputed with all tags stripped before comparing against episode_spoken_words.

word_count_honesty.note: The true tag-stripped spoken count is reported; misreporting is an absolute failure. The prover recomputes and compares against the declared spokenWords.

forbidden_reference_names.note: None of the four reference speakers, their books, or their talks may appear anywhere. The concrete names per style are owned by the style-engine modules and merged over this empty seed at runtime; this config-set does not fabricate them.

forbidden_word_by_style.note: The word paradox never appears in a Counter Intuitive episode. Additional per-style forbidden words are contributed by the style-engine modules and merged over this map.

pure_deliverable.note: The script contains the episode and nothing else: no delivery report, no metadata, no notes.

no_intake_contamination.note: Contact details, consent language, and the image description from the intake payload never appear in the script. The prover compares the script against those intake fields.

$exit_codes.0: PASS: every SACRED band satisfied and every deterministic Tier 1 check clean.
$exit_codes.2: AUTOFAIL: one or more AF-POD-* band or check violations (episode NOT deliverable).
$exit_codes.3: USAGE/IO: missing file or unreadable/invalid JSON (fail-closed).
